name: Colares UFO Attacks
description: The Colares UFO Attacks, also known as Operation Saucer (Operação Prato), refer to a series of alleged UFO sightings and encounters reported in 1977 in the Brazilian island of Colares, near Belém. Residents reported being attacked by beams of light emanating from unidentified flying objects, resulting in burns, paralysis, and blood extraction. The Brazilian Air Force (FAB) conducted an official investigation, dubbed Operation Saucer, led by Captain Uyrangê Hollanda Lima. Proponents claim the investigation documented irrefutable evidence of extraterrestrial activity, including photographic and video evidence, while critics suggest the events were misinterpretations of natural phenomena, psychological effects of mass hysteria, or military exercises. The official documents released to the public are often incomplete, fueling speculation and conspiracy theories. Mainstream science attributes the reported injuries to unknown causes, possibly related to industrial pollution, psychological factors or misidentification of conventional aircraft. The lack of conclusive physical evidence and the incomplete nature of the official investigation contribute to the ongoing debate surrounding the Colares incident.
subtitle: Alleged UFO attacks in Brazil, 1977.

claims.evidence: Witness testimonies describing burns, paralysis, and blood extraction.
claims.criticism: Injuries could be attributed to industrial pollution, psychological factors, or misidentification of known phenomena.

claims.evidence: Leaked documents and testimonies from military personnel involved in Operation Saucer.
claims.criticism: Official documents are incomplete and subject to interpretation. The existence of definitive proof is disputed.

claims.claim: Extraterrestrials were responsible for the attacks.
claims.evidence: Correlation between UFO sightings and reported injuries, the unusual nature of the injuries.
claims.criticism: Lack of verifiable physical evidence linking injuries directly to extraterrestrial technology. Alternative explanations are more plausible.

keyFigures.name: Uyrangê Hollanda Lima
keyFigures.role: Captain leading Operation Saucer. Later died under mysterious circumstances.

keyFigures.name: Jacques Vallée
keyFigures.role: Ufologist who investigated and wrote about the Colares incidents.

relatedConcepts: Close Encounters of the Third Kind

timeline.event: Reported UFO sightings and attacks in Colares.
Brazilian Air Force initiates Operation Saucer.
Captain Uyrangê Hollanda Lima dies under controversial circumstances.
Partial release of Operation Saucer documents, fueling further speculation.

sources.author: Jacques Vallée
sources.work: Passport to Magonia: From Folklore to Flying Saucers
sources.title: Jacques Vallée

sources.author: Olavo Fontes
sources.work: Various articles on Brazilian UFO cases
